source: PDF authoritiesource (pdftotext)
evidence: The words 'Petitioner' and 'Respondent' appear in the opinion caption (after party names) in the PDF but also incorrectly appear orphaned after 'Per Curiam.' in the database text. evidence: The block-quoted text in ¶10 should begin with 'Fair administration' (not 'the Fair administration'). The PDF shows this phrase on a separate indented line following the colon after 'testimony:', and the DB has erroneously inserted 'the' before 'Fair'. This is a clear ingestion artifact.
note: The phrase appears in a block quote in ¶10. The word 'Fair' begins the indented quotation; the article 'the' was inserted incorrectly during text extraction.

old_exact: conducting followed, but test has been scrupulously
new_exact: conducting the test has been scrupulously followed, but
evidence: The DB text is scrambled in a critical part of the block quote in ¶10. The PDF clearly shows 'conducting the test has been scrupulously followed, but' spread across lines 171-172. The DB version is missing 'the test' and has 'followed' in the wrong position, plus has inserted 'test has been scrupulous' which appears to be OCR/extraction confusion with the next sentence.
note: This is a significant textual error in a statutory/regulatory quote. The complete phrase from the Keller precedent is: 'the method approved by the State Toxicologist for conducting the test has been scrupulously followed, but "scrupulous" compliance does not mean...' The DB incorrectly scrambled this to 'conducting followed, but test has been scrupulously "scrupulous"'

source: PDF visual inspection (page 2) + character analysis
evidence: The Greek capital Iota (U+0399) appears as a section divider on line 27, after [¶1] and before [¶2]. The PDF rendering shows a proper Latin Roman numeral 'I', which matches the pattern of 'II' (line 55), 'III' (line 67), and 'IV' (line 101) throughout the document. This is a clear OCR character substitution artifact.
note: This is the only defect found in the database text. All other elements verified correctly: dates (April 8/29, 2022, etc.), citations (N.D.C.C. sections), case names (RTS Shearing, etc.), party names (Gravity/Valence), and document structure.

source: PDF 2025ND30.pdf page-break artifact at end of ¶3
evidence: The PDF shows page break between end of ¶3 (ending with 'appointed.') and start of ¶4 (beginning 'At the pretrial conference, Janachovsky again indicated he was'). The DB text erroneously includes the orphaned fragment 'indicated he was' at the end of ¶3 before starting ¶4. Normalizing both texts and removing page furniture (form-feeds, page numbers) confirms this is the only substantive difference between DB and PDF.

old_exact: At the pretrial conference, Janachovsky again representing himself
new_exact: At the pretrial conference, Janachovsky again indicated he was representing himself
source: PDF 2025ND30.pdf page-break artifact at start of ¶4
evidence: The DB text omits 'indicated he was' due to page-break handling error. The PDF clearly shows the complete phrase 'At the pretrial conference, Janachovsky again indicated he was representing himself'. This proposal and the preceding one address the same page-break artifact split across ¶3/¶4 boundary.
